items.title: Animal Mirror Parade
items.summary: Take turns leading silly animal movements while everyone mirrors the leader.
setupSteps: Clear a small safe space for walking and wiggling.
playSteps: Choose one leader.
The leader moves like an animal while everyone copies.
Switch leaders after three animals.
items.parentNotes: Use gentle movements for younger children and avoid jumping near furniture.
variations: Whisper the animal for a quiet round.
Let each child invent a brand-new animal.

items.title: Couch Cushion Quest
items.summary: A tiny indoor scavenger hunt using safe household clues.
setupSteps: Choose three safe objects in the room.
playSteps: Give one clue at a time.
Children point to or fetch the matching object.
Celebrate each find with a silly cheer.
items.parentNotes: Keep objects visible for younger children.
variations: Use rhyming clues for older children.

items.title: Story Spark Circle
items.summary: Build a family story one sentence at a time.
setupSteps: Sit where everyone can hear each other.
playSteps: Parent starts with: Once there was a tiny dragon who loved pancakes.
Each person adds one sentence.
End when everyone has added three turns.
items.parentNotes: Younger kids can add one word or sound instead of a full sentence.
variations: Make the hero a family pet.
Set the story at bedtime, the park, or a castle.

items.title: Freeze Dance Statues
items.summary: Dance, freeze, and pose as funny statues.
setupSteps: Clear a safe dance space.
playSteps: Parent claps a rhythm or plays family-approved music outside the app.
Everyone dances.
When the parent says freeze, everyone becomes a statue.
items.parentNotes: Keep movement gentle and stop before children get too wild.

items.title: Pillow Boat Adventure
items.summary: Pretend pillows are boats crossing a living-room sea.
setupSteps: Place pillows on the floor with walking space between them.
playSteps: Pretend the floor is water.
Step from boat to boat slowly.
Name friendly sea creatures you see.
items.parentNotes: No jumping. Keep pillows flat and stable.
variations: Make it a quiet tiptoe adventure.

items.title: Calm Cloud Breaths
items.summary: A short breathing reset using imaginary clouds.
setupSteps: Sit or stand comfortably.
playSteps: Pretend to hold a fluffy cloud.
Smell the cloud slowly.
Blow it gently across the sky.
Repeat three times.
items.parentNotes: Keep the tone soft and brief.

items.title: Quiet Color Hunt
items.summary: Find colors silently and point to each one.
setupSteps: Choose a room or sitting spot.
playSteps: Parent names a color softly.
Children silently point to something with that color.
Repeat with five colors.
items.parentNotes: Good for waiting rooms or bedtime transitions.

items.title: Moon Pancake Mission
items.summary: A silly space breakfast story.

madLibs.template: Captain {name} flew a {adjective} rocket to the moon to deliver {food}. When the hatch opened, a moon {animal} shouted {sound}! Everyone laughed and shared breakfast among the stars.

items.title: Dragon Laundry Day
items.summary: A dragon tries to help with chores.

madLibs.template: The {color} dragon found a tiny {object} in the laundry basket. It tried to {verb} it clean, but accidentally made {number} bubbles float through the house.

items.title: Backyard Castle News
items.summary: A royal announcement from a pretend castle.

madLibs.template: Breaking news from the castle of {place}: the royal {adjective} {noun} has been found by a brave {animal}. The kingdom will celebrate with extra giggles tonight.

items.title: If Toys Could Talk
items.summary: Imagine what a favorite toy would say.
items.promptText: Pick a toy nearby. If it could talk for one minute, what would it ask your family?
followUps: What adventure would it want next?

items.title: Tiny Kindness Plan
items.summary: Think of one small kind thing to do today.
items.promptText: What is one tiny kind thing we can do for someone in our family today?
followUps: Can we do it in less than two minutes?

items.title: Family Animal Charades
items.summary: Act out friendly animals while everyone guesses.
setupSteps: Clear a small safe movement space.
playSteps: Parent secretly chooses a friendly animal.
Act it out without words.
Children guess, then take turns acting.
items.parentNotes: For younger kids, whisper the animal and act together.
variations: Use jobs, foods, or storybook creatures instead of animals.

items.title: Silly Would You Rather
items.summary: Choose between two harmless silly options and explain why.
items.promptText: Would you rather have pancakes for shoes or spaghetti for hair for one silly day?
followUps: What would be the funniest part?

items.title: Timed Drawing: Tiny Monster
items.summary: Draw a friendly tiny monster before the timer ends.
setupSteps: Give each child paper and a safe drawing tool.
Set a one-minute timer outside the app or count slowly together.
playSteps: Draw a friendly tiny monster.
Add one silly detail after time ends.
Let each child name their monster.
items.parentNotes: No judging art quality; the goal is fast playful creativity.
variations: Draw a robot, snack creature, or castle pet.

items.title: Sock Skating Rink
items.summary: Slide carefully on socks across a pretend tiny rink.
setupSteps: Use only a smooth, clear floor area and remove obstacles.
playSteps: Stand with feet close and take tiny sliding steps.
Parent calls out slow skating shapes: circle, line, stop.
Finish with a silly statue pose.
items.parentNotes: Skip this on slippery or crowded floors; small steps only.
variations: Pretend to deliver cocoa to stuffed animals.

items.title: Stuffed Animal Rescue
items.summary: Rescue plush friends from safe spots around the room.
setupSteps: Place three stuffed animals in visible, safe places.
playSteps: Parent describes one animal in trouble.
Children walk to rescue it and bring it to base.
Give each rescue a gentle checkup and a name.
items.parentNotes: Keep hiding spots visible for younger children.
variations: Older kids invent the rescue report.

items.title: Kitchen Band Rehearsal
items.summary: Make a gentle rhythm band with safe household items.
setupSteps: Choose non-breakable items and set a soft volume rule.
playSteps: Parent taps a simple beat.
Children copy the beat.
Each child gets one short solo.
End with a quiet final note.
items.parentNotes: No glass, sharp tools, or loud banging.
variations: Play fast, slow, loud, and quiet rounds.

items.title: Blanket Fort Post Office
items.summary: Deliver pretend mail between cozy stations.
setupSteps: Build a simple blanket fort or choose two chairs as stations.
Make a few pretend letters.
playSteps: One child writes or draws a message.
Another delivers it to the fort.
The receiver reads, acts, or guesses the message.
items.parentNotes: Help younger children draw instead of write.
variations: Add kindness notes for family members.

items.title: Teddy Bear Doctor
items.summary: Run a pretend checkup for stuffed animals.
setupSteps: Gather one or two stuffed animals.
playSteps: Ask what the animal needs.
Check ears, paws, and heartbeat with pretend tools.
Prescribe hugs, rest, or a silly song.
items.parentNotes: Keep the tone gentle and reassuring.

items.title: Shape Detective
items.summary: Search the room for circles, squares, and triangles.
setupSteps: Pick a room with visible safe objects.
playSteps: Parent names a shape.
Children point to matching objects.
Count how many shapes you found.
items.parentNotes: Good for waiting or transitions.
variations: Older kids combine shape plus color.

items.title: Pirate Map Steps
items.summary: Follow a tiny pretend map using steps and turns.
setupSteps: Draw a simple map with three stops in one room.
playSteps: Start at the X.
Take tiny steps to each landmark.
At the treasure spot, invent what was found.
items.parentNotes: Keep routes clear and slow.
variations: Let each child draw one stop.

items.title: Whisper Zoo
items.summary: Create a quiet zoo using animal whispers and gentle poses.
setupSteps: Sit or stand in a comfortable spot.
playSteps: Parent names a quiet animal.
Everyone makes its smallest sound.
Hold a gentle animal pose for five seconds.
items.parentNotes: Useful when the room needs to stay calm.

items.title: Rainbow Sort Sprint
items.summary: Sort safe toys or blocks by color in a short timed challenge.
setupSteps: Choose items too large to swallow and clear a sorting space.
playSteps: Pick three colors.
Sort items into color groups.
Count each group together.
items.parentNotes: Avoid small items around toddlers.

items.title: Family News Desk
items.summary: Report happy family news from a pretend desk.
setupSteps: Choose a pretend microphone object or use empty hands.
playSteps: Parent gives a headline: Something kind happened today.
Each child reports one sentence.
End with a pretend weather report for the room.
items.parentNotes: Coach shy kids with sentence starters.

items.title: Five Senses Tour
items.summary: Notice safe sights, sounds, and textures in the room.
setupSteps: Sit together or walk slowly.
playSteps: Name five things you see.
Name four sounds or quiet spots.
Touch three safe textures.
Take one slow breath.
items.parentNotes: Skip taste and smell unless a parent chooses safe options.

items.title: Cleanup Countdown Game
items.summary: Turn a tiny cleanup into a cooperative mission.
setupSteps: Choose a small, safe cleanup zone.
playSteps: Parent picks five items to put away.
Count down from five together.
Celebrate when the tiny mission is complete.
items.parentNotes: Keep it small so it feels successful.

items.title: Paper Airplane Weather
items.summary: Test paper airplanes through gentle pretend weather.
setupSteps: Fold simple paper airplanes and clear a throwing lane.
playSteps: Fly once in calm weather.
Fly once during pretend wind.
Compare which flight went farther.
items.parentNotes: Throw away from faces and fragile items.
variations: Make prediction tickets before each flight.

items.title: Copycat Clap Code
items.summary: Repeat short clap patterns and invent family codes.
setupSteps: Sit where everyone can see hands.
playSteps: Parent claps a two-part pattern.
Children repeat it.
Add one clap each round if everyone is ready.
items.parentNotes: Keep patterns short for younger kids.

items.title: Backyard Micro Safari
items.summary: Look for tiny outdoor details with parent supervision.
setupSteps: Choose a safe yard, porch, or window view.
playSteps: Find one leaf, one shadow, and one tiny movement.
Describe each discovery.
Leave plants and bugs undisturbed.
items.parentNotes: Hands-off observing only unless parent approves.

items.title: Chair Train Station
items.summary: Make chairs into a pretend train and announce stops.
setupSteps: Place stable chairs safely with walking space.
playSteps: Everyone boards the pretend train.
Parent announces the first stop.
At each stop, children name what they see.
items.parentNotes: No climbing or standing on chairs.

items.title: Memory Tray Peek
items.summary: Look at safe objects, hide them, and remember what changed.
setupSteps: Place three to five safe objects on a tray.
playSteps: Everyone looks for ten seconds.
Cover the tray.
Remove one object and reveal the tray.
Guess what changed.
items.parentNotes: Use large objects if toddlers are nearby.

items.title: Emotion Weather Report
items.summary: Describe feelings as weather and choose a next step.
setupSteps: Sit somewhere comfortable.
playSteps: Parent asks: what is your inside weather?
Choose sunny, cloudy, stormy, windy, or calm.
Pick one helpful action: hug, breath, water, quiet, or talk.
items.parentNotes: Do not force sharing; offer choices.

items.title: Tiny Tower Engineers
items.summary: Build a small stable tower and test gentle changes.
setupSteps: Choose safe building pieces and a flat surface.
playSteps: Build the tallest tower that still stands.
Change one thing and test again.
Name the tower design.
items.parentNotes: Keep pieces away from mouths.
variations: Make a team tower with one piece per turn.

items.title: Family Recipe Pretend
items.summary: Invent a silly no-cook recipe out loud.
setupSteps: Sit together. No real cooking required.
playSteps: Pick a pretend dish name.
Each person adds one imaginary ingredient.
Parent reads the recipe back dramatically.
items.parentNotes: Keep food allergy talk gentle if it comes up.

items.title: Hallway Balance Beam
items.summary: Walk a pretend balance line with careful slow steps.
setupSteps: Choose a clear path and identify a pretend line.
playSteps: Walk heel-to-toe slowly.
Freeze at the end.
Return with tiny penguin steps.
items.parentNotes: No running or raised surfaces.
variations: Use a tape line if parent approves.

items.title: Quiet Library Helper
items.summary: Sort books or toys into tiny groups with whisper voices.
setupSteps: Choose a small pile of books or toys.
playSteps: Whisper-sort by size, color, or favorite.
Make one neat stack.
Pick one item to talk about softly.
items.parentNotes: Use only a few items to avoid overwhelm.

items.title: Best Part Today
items.summary: Share one good moment from the day.
items.promptText: What was one good part of today, even if it was tiny?
followUps: Would you want to do it again?

items.title: Helping Superpower
items.summary: Invent a superpower that helps someone.
items.promptText: If you had one helping superpower for ten minutes, what would it do?
followUps: What would your hero name be?

items.title: Kind Robot Instructions
items.summary: Give a robot kind, silly instructions.
items.promptText: A tiny robot wants to be helpful. What three kind jobs should it do?
followUps: What job should it not do?

items.title: Three Word Story
items.summary: Make a story from only three chosen words.
items.promptText: Choose three words and make the shortest story you can with all of them.
followUps: Can someone else add one sentence?
Can you make it gentler or sillier?

items.title: Thank-You Detective
items.summary: Notice someone to thank today.
items.promptText: Who did something helpful or kind today that we can thank?
followUps: How could we say thank you?

items.title: Silly Invention Pitch
items.summary: Pitch a harmless invention for family life.
items.promptText: Invent something silly that would make family life more fun. What is it called?

items.title: Wizard Pajama Parade
items.summary: A sleepy parade gets very silly.

madLibs.template: Professor {name} announced the first {color} pajama parade. A tiny {animal} led the march and shouted {sound} every time someone yawned.
madLibs.readAloudNote: Read it with a sleepy announcer voice.

items.title: Robot Cookie Factory
items.summary: A robot bakes pretend cookies with a surprise.

madLibs.template: The {adjective} robot opened a factory for {food}. It pressed one button and {number} tiny {object}s danced across the counter.

items.title: Undersea Bedtime Bus
items.summary: A bus drives under the sea at bedtime.

madLibs.template: The bedtime bus was driven by a sleepy {animal}. It stopped at {place}, flashed its {color} lights, and whispered {sound} to the fish.

items.title: Castle Pizza Problem
items.summary: A castle feast needs a strange solution.

madLibs.template: The royal {noun} refused to eat the {adjective} {food} until everyone agreed to {verb} around the table one time.
madLibs.readAloudNote: Read it as a royal decree.

items.title: Space Pet Show
items.summary: Pets compete in a zero-gravity show.

madLibs.template: Judge {name} welcomed the space {animal} show. The winner floated {adverb} through the ring and made a proud {sound}.

items.title: Breakfast Dragon Train
items.summary: A dragon train delivers breakfast.

madLibs.template: The {color} dragon train delivered {food} to the {place}. It rang its bell {number} times and served everyone a plate of giggles.
madLibs.readAloudNote: Make the train bell sound gentle.
